hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 3, 5, Ace. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.
River — Jack. Your opponent shoved all-in without hesitation.

wrongReasons: K 5 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with K 5.
2 2 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only two pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
2 A doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only two pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 10 5

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
